function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'n < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX / 2' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'n < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX / 2' always executed?
dominators: Does the statement line 7:  <statement> for( i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX; i++ ) </statement> dominate line 4:  <statement> int main( int ac, char *av[] ) </statement>?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 13:  <statement> for( n = 1; n < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX; n++ ) { </statement> is reached, the statement line 6:  <statement> int n, n2, i; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does variable n at line 13: <statement>     for( n = 1; n < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX; n++ ) { </statement> determine the value stored in variable n2 at line 14: <statement>         n2 = array[n]; </statement>?

Does variable ac at line 4: <statement> int main( int ac, char *av[] ) </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 7: <statement>     for( i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX; i++ ) </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'n2' live at the end of function 'main'?
Does variable 'i' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Is the statement 'for( i = 2; i * n < ARRAY_SIZE_MAX; i++ )' unreachable?
Is this statement not dead: “printf( "%d %d\n", n, n2 );”?
